name: Arithmetic Asian call via Monte Carlo
task: Create /tmp/eval_asian/asian.py with asian_call_mc(S0, K, T, r, sigma, n_steps, n_paths, seed=42). Simulate geometric-Brownian-motion paths with n_steps observations per path; for each path compute the arithmetic average of the n_steps prices, then the payoff max(avg - K, 0); discount the mean by exp(-rT). Use random.Random(seed) so results are reproducible. Pure Python (math + random).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_asian/asian.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); A = mod.asian_call_mc; price = A(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2, 50, 2000, seed=42); assert 4 < price < 8, f'asian {price}'; p1 = A(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2, 50, 1000, seed=7); p2 = A(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2, 50, 1000, seed=7); assert p1 == p2; print('OK')
